rawText:
Across an extraordinarily diverse range of disciplines -- from psychiatry and agriculture to battery engineering and nuclear physics -- scientific frontiers are advancing in ways that challenge conventional assumptions about what is possible, suggesting that the next decade could reshape everything from how we treat mental illness to how we power civilisation itself.

In the realm of mental health, a pilot trial conducted by Valerie Taylor at the University of Calgary has demonstrated that faecal microbiota transplants are safe and tolerable for patients with bipolar disorder, opening the door to larger studies that could exploit the gut-brain axis -- a communication network comprising the vagus nerve, the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, immune pathways, and bacterial metabolites like short-chain fatty acids -- as a therapeutic target for the estimated 37 million people worldwide who suffer from the condition.

The trial's most intriguing puzzle was that patients who received their own stool back showed improvements comparable to those who received a healthy donor's microbiome, raising the possibility that the bowel cleanse and reinfusion process itself -- a kind of biological ploughing, as one researcher described it -- may reset the gut ecosystem in ways that influence brain chemistry through reduced inflammation and restored short-chain fatty acid production.

Meanwhile, in the fields and laboratories where humanity's food supply is engineered, CRISPR gene editing is enabling alterations that conventional breeding could never achieve -- from seedless blackberries and stoneless cherries to non-browning avocados and tomatoes made thirty percent sweeter by disabling genes that limit sugar production -- while artificial intelligence accelerates the process by modelling how multiple genes, biochemical pathways, and environmental factors interact to produce complex traits like flavour.

The commercialisation of gene-edited fruits has been slow, largely because tree crops such as apples and peaches require several years to bear fruit from an altered seed, but regulatory momentum is building as countries from Argentina to the European Union move to treat CRISPR-edited products no differently from conventionally bred ones -- a shift that could dramatically expand the market, much as seedless easy-peel mandarins increased the value of America's entire citrus market by roughly a third within four years of becoming widely available.

On the energy front, the electric vehicle industry stands on the cusp of eliminating one of its most persistent obstacles -- slow charging times -- as companies like BYD and Cambridge-spun Nyobolt develop batteries whose redesigned electrodes allow lithium ions to flow with dramatically reduced internal resistance, enabling a charge from ten percent to seventy percent capacity in as little as five minutes.

The durability concern that has traditionally accompanied fast charging is also being addressed, with Nyobolt reporting that its niobium-tungsten-oxide anodes retain more than eighty percent of battery capacity after 4,000 rapid-charging cycles -- equivalent to approximately one million kilometres of driving -- a figure that, if validated at scale, would make the leisurely coffee stop at a charging station an artefact of an earlier technological era.

Perhaps the most ambitious frontier of all is nuclear fusion, where China's state-led programme -- anchored by the Burning Plasma Experimental Superconducting Tokamak under construction in Hefei and scheduled for completion by 2027 -- has emerged as a serious contender through a three-pronged strategy of setting clear research priorities, providing vast funding, and building an industrial supply chain for the cutting-edge components that tokamaks demand.

China's timeline, which envisions the China Fusion Engineering Demo Reactor operational by 2030 and grid-connected power stations to follow, runs at least a decade ahead of other governmental efforts, though seventy-seven private fusion startups worldwide -- having collectively raised fifteen billion dollars to pursue approaches ranging from advanced tokamaks to laser-driven designs and stellarators -- could yet leapfrog these expensive public programmes.

What unites these disparate scientific advances is the recognition that transformative breakthroughs often depend less on a single eureka moment than on the patient convergence of engineering ingenuity, institutional commitment, and the willingness to learn by doing -- whether that means resetting a patient's gut microbiome, redesigning a battery electrode at the molecular level, editing a single gene in a blackberry, or confining plasma at temperatures exceeding 150 million degrees Celsius inside a doughnut-shaped reactor.

overview.title: Science at the Edge: From Gut Bacteria to Fusion Reactors

sentences.original: Across an extraordinarily diverse range of disciplines -- from psychiatry and agriculture to battery engineering and nuclear physics -- scientific frontiers are advancing in ways that challenge conventional assumptions about what is possible, suggesting that the next decade could reshape everything from how we treat mental illness to how we power civilisation itself.
sentences.translation: 정신의학과 농업에서 배터리 공학과 핵물리학에 이르기까지, 극도로 다양한 학문 분야에 걸쳐 과학의 최전선이 기존의 통념에 도전하는 방식으로 전진하고 있으며, 향후 10년이 정신 질환 치료 방식에서 문명 자체에 동력을 공급하는 방식까지 모든 것을 바꿔놓을 수 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: Across an extraordinarily diverse range of disciplines
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 부사어 (범위)

grammarElements.text: scientific frontiers
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are advancing
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 동사

grammarElements.text: in ways that challenge conventional assumptions
grammarElements.label: 관계절 수식 전치사구

grammarElements.text: suggesting that the next decade could reshape everything
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (부연 설명)

vocabulary.example: Conventional wisdom held that such a feat was impossible.
Quantum computing remains a scientific frontier with vast unknowns.

expressions.usage: 새로운 발견이 기존 상식을 뒤흔들 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 광범위한 범위를 먼저 제시한 뒤 그 안에서 일어나는 변화를 설명하는 도입 패턴
examples: Across multiple industries, companies are adopting AI in ways that transform daily operations.
Across all age groups, consumers are shifting in ways that surprise researchers.

drillQuestions.question: Scientific frontiers are advancing in ways that challenge ______ assumptions about what is possible.

sentences.notes: 기사 전체를 여는 총괄 문장으로, from A to B 병렬구가 두 번 반복되어 주제의 폭을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Across an extraordinarily diverse range of disciplines

sentences.original: In the realm of mental health, a pilot trial conducted by Valerie Taylor at the University of Calgary has demonstrated that faecal microbiota transplants are safe and tolerable for patients with bipolar disorder, opening the door to larger studies that could exploit the gut-brain axis -- a communication network comprising the vagus nerve, the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, immune pathways, and bacterial metabolites like short-chain fatty acids -- as a therapeutic target for the estimated 37 million people worldwide who suffer from the condition.
sentences.translation: 정신건강 영역에서, 캘거리대학교의 밸러리 테일러가 수행한 예비 시험은 분변 미생물군 이식이 양극성 장애 환자에게 안전하고 견딜 만하다는 것을 입증했으며, 미주신경·시상하부-뇌하수체-부신 축·면역 경로·단쇄지방산 같은 세균 대사산물로 구성된 소통 네트워크인 장-뇌 축을 전 세계 약 3,700만 명의 환자를 위한 치료 표적으로 활용할 수 있는 대규모 연구의 문을 열었다.

grammarElements.text: a pilot trial conducted by Valerie Taylor
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 과거분사 수식

grammarElements.text: has demonstrated
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: that faecal microbiota transplants are safe and tolerable
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: opening the door to larger studies
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Researchers hope to exploit this pathway for new treatments.

expressions.usage: 새로운 연구·기회가 시작될 가능성을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Study/Trial] has demonstrated that [finding], opening the door to [possibility]
patterns.explanation: 연구 결과를 제시한 뒤 그것이 열어주는 후속 가능성을 연결하는 학술 서술 패턴
examples: The experiment demonstrated that the compound is stable, opening the door to clinical trials.
The survey demonstrated that demand exists, opening the door to commercial launch.

drillQuestions.question: A pilot trial has demonstrated that faecal microbiota transplants are safe and ______ for patients with bipolar disorder.

sentences.notes: 의학 용어가 밀집된 고난도 문장. gut-brain axis를 삽입절로 풀어 설명하는 구조에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: a pilot trial conducted by Valerie Taylor
opening the door to larger studies

sentences.original: The trial's most intriguing puzzle was that patients who received their own stool back showed improvements comparable to those who received a healthy donor's microbiome, raising the possibility that the bowel cleanse and reinfusion process itself -- a kind of biological ploughing, as one researcher described it -- may reset the gut ecosystem in ways that influence brain chemistry through reduced inflammation and restored short-chain fatty acid production.
sentences.translation: 이 시험에서 가장 흥미로운 수수께끼는, 자기 자신의 대변을 되돌려 받은 환자들이 건강한 기증자의 미생물군을 받은 환자들과 비슷한 개선을 보였다는 점이었으며, 이는 장 세척과 재주입 과정 자체가 — 한 연구자의 표현대로 일종의 생물학적 쟁기질이 — 염증을 줄이고 단쇄지방산 생산을 복원함으로써 뇌 화학에 영향을 미치는 방식으로 장내 생태계를 재설정할 수 있다는 가능성을 제기했다.

grammarElements.text: The trial's most intriguing puzzle
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: was
grammarElements.label: 연결동사

grammarElements.text: that patients who received their own stool back showed improvements comparable to those who received a healthy donor's microbiome
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (보어)

grammarElements.text: raising the possibility that ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과/부연)

vocabulary.example: The most intriguing result was entirely unexpected.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못한 결과가 새로운 가설을 열 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 실험 결과에서 새로운 가설로 이어지는 과학적 추론 패턴
examples: The data raised the possibility that stress may alter immune function.
The anomaly raised the possibility that dark energy may fluctuate over time.

drillQuestions.question: The trial's most ______ puzzle was that patients who received their own stool back showed comparable improvements.

sentences.notes: 'biological ploughing'이라는 비유가 장 세척 과정을 농경의 쟁기질에 빗대어 직관적으로 설명한다.

sentences.original: Meanwhile, in the fields and laboratories where humanity's food supply is engineered, CRISPR gene editing is enabling alterations that conventional breeding could never achieve -- from seedless blackberries and stoneless cherries to non-browning avocados and tomatoes made thirty percent sweeter by disabling genes that limit sugar production -- while artificial intelligence accelerates the process by modelling how multiple genes, biochemical pathways, and environmental factors interact to produce complex traits like flavour.
sentences.translation: 한편, 인류의 식량 공급이 설계되는 농장과 연구실에서 크리스퍼 유전자 편집은 기존 품종 개량으로는 결코 이룰 수 없었던 변형을 가능하게 하고 있다 — 씨 없는 블랙베리와 씨앗 없는 체리에서부터 갈변하지 않는 아보카도, 당 생산을 제한하는 유전자를 비활성화해 30퍼센트 더 달게 만든 토마토에 이르기까지 — 동시에 인공지능은 여러 유전자, 생화학 경로, 환경 요인이 풍미 같은 복합 형질을 만들기 위해 어떻게 상호작용하는지를 모델링함으로써 이 과정을 가속하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: CRISPR gene editing
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is enabling
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 동사

grammarElements.text: alterations that conventional breeding could never achieve
grammarElements.label: 관계절 수식 목적어

grammarElements.text: while artificial intelligence accelerates the process
grammarElements.label: while 대조/동시 접속절

vocabulary.example: Genetic alterations can improve crop resilience to drought.

expressions.usage: 기술이나 방법이 기존 절차를 빠르게 만들 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Technology] is enabling [outcomes] that [old method] could never achieve
patterns.explanation: 신기술이 구기술의 한계를 넘어선 성과를 가능케 했음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: AI is enabling diagnoses that human screening could never achieve.
3D printing is enabling designs that traditional machining could never achieve.

drillQuestions.question: CRISPR gene editing is enabling alterations that conventional breeding could never ______.

sentences.notes: from A to B 나열과 while 절의 병렬 구조로 CRISPR와 AI의 시너지를 압축적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: CRISPR gene editing is enabling alterations
koreanAlignment.ko: 크리스퍼 유전자 편집은 변형을 가능하게 하고 있다
기존 품종 개량으로는 결코 이룰 수 없었던

sentences.original: The commercialisation of gene-edited fruits has been slow, largely because tree crops such as apples and peaches require several years to bear fruit from an altered seed, but regulatory momentum is building as countries from Argentina to the European Union move to treat CRISPR-edited products no differently from conventionally bred ones -- a shift that could dramatically expand the market, much as seedless easy-peel mandarins increased the value of America's entire citrus market by roughly a third within four years of becoming widely available.
sentences.translation: 유전자 편집 과일의 상용화는 느리게 진행되어 왔는데, 주로 사과나 복숭아 같은 수목 작물이 변형된 씨앗에서 열매를 맺기까지 수년이 걸리기 때문이지만, 아르헨티나에서 유럽연합에 이르기까지 각국이 크리스퍼 편집 제품을 기존 품종 개량 제품과 동일하게 취급하는 방향으로 움직이면서 규제의 추진력이 형성되고 있다 — 이는 씨 없는 이지필 만다린이 널리 보급된 지 4년 만에 미국 전체 감귤 시장의 가치를 대략 3분의 1 정도 끌어올린 것처럼 시장을 극적으로 확장할 수 있는 전환이다.

grammarElements.text: The commercialisation of gene-edited fruits
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has been slow
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 보어 구조

grammarElements.text: largely because tree crops ... require several years to bear fruit
grammarElements.label: 이유의 because 절

grammarElements.text: but regulatory momentum is building
grammarElements.label: 역접의 but 절

grammarElements.text: much as seedless easy-peel mandarins increased the value
grammarElements.label: 비교의 much as 절

vocabulary.example: The commercialisation of the drug took longer than expected.

expressions.usage: 변화의 힘이 점점 커지고 있을 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Process] has been slow, largely because [reason], but [counter-trend] is building
patterns.explanation: 지연 원인을 인정한 뒤 반전 흐름을 제시하는 균형 잡힌 분석 패턴
examples: Adoption has been slow, largely because costs remain high, but interest is building.
Reform has been slow, largely because stakeholders resist change, but pressure is building.

drillQuestions.question: The commercialisation of gene-edited fruits has been slow, largely because tree crops require several years to bear ______.

sentences.notes: much as 비교절로 과거 유사 사례(만다린)를 끌어와 미래 전망의 근거로 삼는 논증 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: The commercialisation of gene-edited fruits has been slow
koreanAlignment.ko: 유전자 편집 과일의 상용화는 느리게 진행되어 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: within four years of becoming widely available

sentences.original: On the energy front, the electric vehicle industry stands on the cusp of eliminating one of its most persistent obstacles -- slow charging times -- as companies like BYD and Cambridge-spun Nyobolt develop batteries whose redesigned electrodes allow lithium ions to flow with dramatically reduced internal resistance, enabling a charge from ten percent to seventy percent capacity in as little as five minutes.
sentences.translation: 에너지 전선에서, 전기차 산업은 가장 끈질긴 장애물 중 하나인 느린 충전 시간을 제거하는 길목에 서 있다. BYD와 케임브리지에서 분사한 나이오볼트 같은 기업들이 재설계된 전극으로 리튬 이온이 극적으로 줄어든 내부 저항으로 흐를 수 있는 배터리를 개발하여, 불과 5분 만에 10퍼센트에서 70퍼센트까지 충전이 가능하게 만들고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: the electric vehicle industry
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: stands on the cusp of
grammarElements.label: 동사구 (관용표현)

grammarElements.text: eliminating one of its most persistent obstacles
grammarElements.label: 동명사 목적어

grammarElements.text: enabling a charge from ten percent to seventy percent capacity
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The new electrode design improves energy density significantly.
Inflation has been a persistent challenge for policymakers.

expressions.usage: 중대한 변화가 바로 코앞에 다가왔을 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 어떤 산업이 오랜 장애물을 극복하기 직전이라는 긴장감을 전달하는 패턴
examples: The pharmaceutical industry stands on the cusp of eliminating malaria.
Aviation stands on the cusp of achieving carbon-neutral flight.

drillQuestions.question: The electric vehicle industry stands on the ______ of eliminating one of its most persistent obstacles.

sentences.notes: stands on the cusp of라는 표현이 기술 혁신의 임박함을 생생하게 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: stands on the cusp of eliminating
in as little as five minutes

sentences.original: The durability concern that has traditionally accompanied fast charging is also being addressed, with Nyobolt reporting that its niobium-tungsten-oxide anodes retain more than eighty percent of battery capacity after 4,000 rapid-charging cycles -- equivalent to approximately one million kilometres of driving -- a figure that, if validated at scale, would make the leisurely coffee stop at a charging station an artefact of an earlier technological era.
sentences.translation: 급속 충전에 전통적으로 수반되어 온 내구성 우려도 해소되고 있는데, 나이오볼트는 자사의 니오븀-텅스텐-산화물 양극이 4,000회 급속충전 사이클 이후에도 배터리 용량의 80퍼센트 이상을 유지한다고 보고했다 — 이는 약 100만 킬로미터 주행에 해당하며, 대규모로 검증될 경우 충전소에서의 여유로운 커피 한잔이 이전 기술 시대의 유물이 될 수치이다.

grammarElements.text: The durability concern that has traditionally accompanied fast charging
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 수식

grammarElements.text: is also being addressed
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 수동태

grammarElements.text: with Nyobolt reporting that ...
grammarElements.label: with + 분사 부대상황 구문

grammarElements.text: a figure that, if validated at scale, would make ...
grammarElements.label: 가정법 관계절

vocabulary.example: Dial-up internet is now an artefact of the 1990s.

expressions.usage: 실험실 결과가 실제 대량 생산에서도 통할지 조건을 붙일 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Concern] is being addressed, with [company] reporting that [data]
patterns.explanation: 기존 우려를 인정하면서 구체적 데이터로 해소 상황을 제시하는 패턴
examples: The safety concern is being addressed, with Tesla reporting that crash rates have dropped.
The cost issue is being addressed, with SpaceX reporting that launch prices have halved.

drillQuestions.question: Nyobolt's anodes retain more than eighty percent of battery capacity after 4,000 rapid-charging ______.

sentences.notes: leisurely coffee stop이라는 일상적 이미지로 기술 변혁의 체감 효과를 전달하는 서술이 인상적이다.

koreanAlignment.en: equivalent to approximately one million kilometres of driving
the leisurely coffee stop at a charging station

sentences.original: Perhaps the most ambitious frontier of all is nuclear fusion, where China's state-led programme -- anchored by the Burning Plasma Experimental Superconducting Tokamak under construction in Hefei and scheduled for completion by 2027 -- has emerged as a serious contender through a three-pronged strategy of setting clear research priorities, providing vast funding, and building an industrial supply chain for the cutting-edge components that tokamaks demand.
sentences.translation: 아마도 모든 최전선 중 가장 야심 찬 것은 핵융합일 것이다. 이 분야에서 중국의 국가 주도 프로그램은 — 허페이에서 건설 중이며 2027년 완공 예정인 '연소 플라스마 실험 초전도 토카막'을 중심으로 — 명확한 연구 우선순위 설정, 방대한 자금 투입, 토카막이 요구하는 첨단 부품을 위한 산업 공급망 구축이라는 삼각 전략을 통해 강력한 경쟁자로 부상했다.

grammarElements.text: Perhaps the most ambitious frontier of all
grammarElements.label: 주어 (최상급 강조)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 연결동사

grammarElements.text: nuclear fusion
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: where China's state-led programme ... has emerged as a serious contender
grammarElements.label: 관계부사 where 절

vocabulary.example: China has emerged as a serious contender in the fusion race.

expressions.usage: 전략이 세 가지 핵심 축으로 구성되어 있음을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Entity] has emerged as a serious contender through [strategy]
patterns.explanation: 주체가 특정 전략을 통해 주요 경쟁자로 부상했음을 설명하는 패턴
examples: India has emerged as a serious contender through investment in digital infrastructure.
The startup has emerged as a serious contender through aggressive pricing.

drillQuestions.question: China's programme has emerged as a serious contender through a three-______ strategy.

sentences.notes: 삽입절 구조로 토카막의 정식 명칭과 일정을 밀어넣은 뒤, through 이하에서 전략의 세 축을 병렬 나열한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Perhaps the most ambitious frontier of all
koreanAlignment.ko: 아마도 모든 최전선 중 가장 야심 찬 것

koreanAlignment.en: has emerged as a serious contender

sentences.original: China's timeline, which envisions the China Fusion Engineering Demo Reactor operational by 2030 and grid-connected power stations to follow, runs at least a decade ahead of other governmental efforts, though seventy-seven private fusion startups worldwide -- having collectively raised fifteen billion dollars to pursue approaches ranging from advanced tokamaks to laser-driven designs and stellarators -- could yet leapfrog these expensive public programmes.
sentences.translation: 2030년까지 중국 핵융합 공학 시범 원자로를 가동하고 이후 전력망 연결 발전소를 추진하겠다는 중국의 일정은 다른 정부 차원의 노력보다 최소 10년은 앞서 있지만, 첨단 토카막에서 레이저 구동 설계와 스텔러레이터에 이르는 다양한 접근법을 추구하며 총 150억 달러를 모금한 전 세계 77개 민간 핵융합 스타트업들이 이 값비싼 공공 프로그램을 뛰어넘을 가능성은 여전히 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: China's timeline
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which envisions ... power stations to follow
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: runs at least a decade ahead of
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: though seventy-seven private fusion startups ... could yet leapfrog
grammarElements.label: 양보의 though 절

vocabulary.example: Smaller firms could leapfrog established players with disruptive technology.

expressions.usage: 일정이나 진행 속도가 다른 주체보다 빠를 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Timeline] runs [duration] ahead of [others], though [alternative] could yet [verb]
patterns.explanation: 한쪽의 우위를 인정하면서도 역전 가능성을 열어두는 균형 잡힌 분석 패턴
examples: The EU's plan runs two years ahead of Britain's, though private investment could yet close the gap.
Their schedule runs months ahead of rivals, though a regulatory setback could yet erase the lead.

drillQuestions.question: Seventy-seven private fusion startups could yet ______ these expensive public programmes.

sentences.notes: though 절이 중국 우위론에 민간 스타트업이라는 변수를 대비시켜 분석의 균형을 잡는다.

koreanAlignment.en: runs at least a decade ahead of other governmental efforts
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 정부 노력보다 최소 10년은 앞서 있다

koreanAlignment.en: having collectively raised fifteen billion dollars

sentences.original: What unites these disparate scientific advances is the recognition that transformative breakthroughs often depend less on a single eureka moment than on the patient convergence of engineering ingenuity, institutional commitment, and the willingness to learn by doing -- whether that means resetting a patient's gut microbiome, redesigning a battery electrode at the molecular level, editing a single gene in a blackberry, or confining plasma at temperatures exceeding 150 million degrees Celsius inside a doughnut-shaped reactor.
sentences.translation: 이 서로 다른 과학적 진보를 하나로 묶는 것은, 혁신적 돌파구가 단 한 번의 유레카 순간보다 공학적 독창성, 제도적 헌신, 그리고 실행을 통해 배우려는 의지가 인내심 있게 수렴되는 데에 더 많이 의존한다는 인식이다 — 그것이 환자의 장내 미생물군 재설정이든, 분자 수준의 배터리 전극 재설계든, 블랙베리의 유전자 하나를 편집하는 것이든, 도넛 모양 원자로 안에서 섭씨 1억 5천만 도를 넘는 온도로 플라스마를 가두는 것이든 말이다.

grammarElements.text: What unites these disparate scientific advances
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사 what 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 연결동사

grammarElements.text: the recognition that transformative breakthroughs often depend less on ... than on ...
grammarElements.label: 보어 (less ... than 비교구조)

grammarElements.text: whether that means ... or ...
grammarElements.label: whether A or B 양보절

vocabulary.example: The convergence of technology and biology is creating new medical tools.
The solution required both ingenuity and patience.

expressions.phrase: depend less on A than on B
expressions.usage: 흔히 생각하는 요인보다 진짜 요인을 강조할 때 쓰는 비교 표현

patterns.pattern: What unites [diverse items] is the recognition that [insight]
patterns.explanation: 다양한 사례를 관통하는 공통 통찰을 제시하며 글을 마무리하는 종합 패턴
examples: What unites these reforms is the recognition that growth requires stability.
What unites these failures is the recognition that complexity was underestimated.

drillQuestions.question: Transformative breakthroughs often depend less on a single eureka moment than on the patient ______ of engineering ingenuity.

sentences.notes: 기사 전체를 관통하는 주제를 마무리하는 종합 문장. whether A or B or C or D 나열로 앞선 네 분야를 한 번에 회수한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What unites these disparate scientific advances
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 서로 다른 과학적 진보를 하나로 묶는 것
공학적 독창성이 인내심 있게 수렴되는 것

pronunciationNotes: ingenuity /ˌɪndʒəˈnjuːəti/ — 강세가 세 번째 음절 '-nu-'에 온다
